chief_complaint: A 47-year-old male with type 2 diabetes presented with intractable vomiting and Kussmaul respiration.
history: The patient had a history of type 2 diabetes and presented with severe metabolic acidosis, intractable vomiting, and Kussmaul respiration.
exam: Severe metabolic acidosis (pH 6.98), intractable vomiting, Kussmaul respiration
labs: Chest computed tomography (CT) revealed a 2.5 x 4.0 cm pneumopericardium and a 3-mm linear defect at the left ventricular apex. Transthoracic echocardiography excluded cardiac tamponade.
correct_diagnosis: Diabetic ketoacidosis with pneumopericardium and pericardial tear
differentials: Diabetic ketoacidosis with pneumopericardium and pericardial tear
correct_ranking: Diabetic ketoacidosis with pneumopericardium and pericardial tear
